name: Novosibirsk Oblast (Russia)

colors.symbolism: courage and the heroism of the people of Novosibirsk
purity, devotion and the Siberian winter
the Ob River and the many rivers and lakes of the region
hope, abundance and the natural wealth of the land
the sable, a Siberian heraldic animal
the bread and salt of Russian hospitality

description: Five vertical stripes of red, white, blue, white and green; in the centre, two black sables hold up a golden round loaf of bread crowned with a salt cellar.
history: Adopted on 29 July 2003 by the Novosibirsk Oblast Council of Deputies; the design was confirmed by a 2007 oblast law on state symbols.

features.symbolism: red recalls the courage and memory of the people of Novosibirsk; white the purity and the Siberian winter; blue the Ob River and the lakes; green the abundance of the Siberian land.
two black sables, the heraldic animals of Siberia, holding aloft a loaf of bread and salt — the traditional Russian symbol of hospitality.
a golden korovai (round loaf) topped with a salt shaker, symbolising hospitality and the abundance of the Siberian land.
